cards.title: Java Spring Boot actuator control plane exposure
cards.trigger: Spring Boot with actuator, management.endpoints, /actuator, /actuator/mappings, /actuator/env, /actuator/heapdump, or Whitelabel error clues.
cards.first_safe_check: Fetch only the actuator index and mappings/env availability with headers/status/length; do not dump secrets unless closure requires a specific key location.
cards.oracle: Actuator endpoint list, mappings JSON, env key names, heapdump availability, or auth boundary differential.
cards.stop_rule: Stop actuator branch if index and common endpoints are consistently 404/403 with no Spring error/control-plane differential.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to config/source map or Spring Security route matcher if actuator is blocked but Spring Boot remains confirmed.
cards.snippet: Actuator is a direct closure/control-plane family; prove exposure and auth before attempting sensitive reads.
cards.confirm: A management endpoint returns endpoint data, route mappings, env key names, heapdump/logfile, or a meaningful auth differential.
cards.falsify: No actuator-shaped endpoint responds and source/config has no actuator exposure signal.
failure_modes: Ignoring mappings as a route oracle

cards.title: Java H2 console and datasource closure
cards.trigger: jdbc:h2, spring.h2.console.enabled, /h2-console, H2 dependency, datasource URL, or H2 parser mismatch clue.
cards.first_safe_check: Verify /h2-console exposure and datasource URL/credential key names from config; use non-destructive login/metadata checks first.
cards.oracle: H2 console page, JDBC URL from config, successful metadata query, or parser/error differential.
cards.stop_rule: Stop if H2 is test-only/unreachable and no console/JDBC route exists.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to SQL/HQL/MyBatis source slices when H2 is only the backend parser but console is closed.
cards.snippet: H2 often gives direct database/admin/flag closure when console and credentials are exposed; otherwise treat as parser/backend evidence.
cards.confirm: H2 console or JDBC-backed query oracle is reachable with known/config-derived connection settings.
cards.falsify: No console route and all SQL routes/config evidence point to non-H2 runtime.

cards.title: Java MyBatis raw ${} SQL injection
cards.trigger: MyBatis mapper XML or annotations contain ${}, dynamic SQL, @Select/@Update, mapper namespace, or suspicious string substitution.
cards.first_safe_check: Trace controller/service to the mapper and test one harmless boolean or quote differential on the exact mapped parameter.
cards.oracle: SQL syntax/error difference, boolean row-count/body difference, logged constructed SQL, or local harness constructed query.
cards.stop_rule: Stop SQLi payload depth if the parameter uses #{} or is not route-controlled after tracing.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to auth/logic or H2/HQL parser mismatch if mapper is reachable but input is transformed/whitelisted.
cards.snippet: MyBatis ${} is source-proven only after route-controlled parameter reaches that mapper argument.
cards.confirm: A one-variable mapped parameter changes SQL semantics or error in the expected way.
cards.falsify: Mapper path is unreachable, parameter is not controlled, or sink uses safe binding only.

cards.title: Java Thymeleaf or SpEL template-name expression injection
cards.trigger: Thymeleaf/Spring view names, fragment expressions, SpEL parser, ModelAndView, setViewName, or controller returns user-influenced view string.
cards.first_safe_check: Identify template engine/context and test a harmless engine-specific expression marker or template resolution error with one controlled field.
cards.oracle: Expression evaluation result, template resolver error, fragment parsing differential, or local harness render output.
cards.stop_rule: Stop SSTI if the engine/context is not confirmed or the controlled value is rendered only as data.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to file read via Spring/JDK utilities when expression evaluation exists but command execution is blocked/unneeded.
cards.snippet: Template-name and expression bugs need engine/context proof; prefer file-read closure over shell-first thinking.
cards.confirm: Harmless expression marker evaluates or produces an engine-specific parse differential.
cards.falsify: Value is escaped data only or never reaches template resolution/expression parser.

cards.title: Java upload or file-write to JSP/static writeback
cards.trigger: MultipartFile.transferTo, Part/FileItem upload, FileOutputStream/Files.write, ServletContext.getRealPath, static resource path, or upload extension blacklist.
cards.first_safe_check: Upload/write a harmless canary filename and verify storage path, readback, served status, create/overwrite behavior, and extension/content-type normalization.
cards.oracle: Canary appears in filesystem/readback/static route or path traversal changes storage location.
cards.stop_rule: Stop JSP/writeback chain if path is not served/reloaded and no file-read or include consumer exists.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to file read/path traversal or archive extraction if write exists but execution/readback does not.
cards.snippet: Write primitives require served/reload/readback proof before JSP execution assumptions.
cards.confirm: Canary write and retrieval or filesystem evidence prove controllable write location.
cards.falsify: Upload stores outside served/consumed path with fixed safe filename and no traversal/readback.

cards.title: Java Shiro rememberMe gated deserialization
cards.trigger: Shiro dependency/config, rememberMe cookie, deleteMe oracle, CookieRememberMeManager, cipherKey, or shiro.ini filter chain.
cards.first_safe_check: Confirm rememberMe/deleteMe behavior and key/config source before any gadget payload; map classpath for actual gadget support.
cards.oracle: deleteMe cookie, Shiro parse error, changed auth/session state, or controlled benign serialized object handling.
cards.stop_rule: Stop gadget attempts if no key/default-key proof, no rememberMe parser oracle, or no gadget classpath.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to Shiro filter-chain/authz bypass when rememberMe crypto/parser gates fail.
cards.snippet: Shiro rememberMe is a gated branch; dependency alone is never enough.
cards.confirm: rememberMe parsing behavior changes in a Shiro-specific way under a controlled benign test.
cards.falsify: No rememberMe behavior, no usable key evidence, or no gadget classpath after app mapping.

cards.title: Java Spring Security route matcher or method confusion
cards.trigger: SecurityFilterChain, antMatchers/mvcMatchers/requestMatchers, permitAll, hasRole, method security, path semicolon, encoded slash, or admin route 403.
cards.first_safe_check: Build a route-auth table and run one path/method/semicolon/trailing-slash differential against a blocked high-value route.
cards.oracle: 403/302/200 auth boundary difference, route handler change, or method security mismatch.
cards.stop_rule: Stop normalization variants after two flat differentials with no route/auth change.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to mass assignment/session role logic if route matching is stable.
cards.snippet: Route matcher confusion is often shorter than RCE in Java Web CTFs.
cards.confirm: One route/method/path variant changes auth decision or reaches a different handler.
cards.falsify: All focused variants preserve the same auth decision and handler.

cards.title: Java Spring DTO mass assignment to admin or ownership state
cards.trigger: @RequestBody, @ModelAttribute, entity binding, BeanUtils.copyProperties, role/isAdmin/userId/owner fields, registration/profile/update endpoints.
cards.first_safe_check: Compare UI fields to DTO/entity fields and add one harmless privileged-looking field in a controlled request with clean-session oracle.
cards.oracle: Stored field appears, role/owner/authz behavior changes, or response serializes unexpected bound field.
cards.stop_rule: Stop if binder explicitly whitelists fields or extra fields are ignored with no stored/behavior differential.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to IDOR/authz matrix if ownership fields exist but cannot be mass-assigned.
cards.snippet: Mass assignment often gives admin/owner closure with less risk than gadget chains.
cards.confirm: Extra bound field is stored or affects authorization/session behavior.
cards.falsify: Extra fields are rejected/ignored and no state changes occur.

cards.title: Java Fastjson or Jackson polymorphic parser gated branch
cards.trigger: fastjson, JSON.parseObject, @type, Jackson ObjectMapper default typing, activateDefaultTyping, JsonTypeInfo, or parser exception differentials.
cards.first_safe_check: Confirm the exact parser and controlled JSON field/body reaches polymorphic/type handling with a harmless type/error differential.
cards.oracle: Parser-specific exception, accepted type metadata, different response/error class, or local harness parse result.
cards.stop_rule: Stop gadget depth if parser is framework-safe binding only, type metadata is ignored, or route is unreachable.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to source-proven business sinks or auth logic when parser gates fail.
cards.snippet: JSON parser dependencies become exploitable only with controlled type parsing and classpath/oracle proof.
cards.confirm: A benign type metadata probe produces parser-specific behavior.
cards.falsify: No controlled parser path or type metadata ignored under controlled conditions.

cards.title: Java XXE through XML parser feature gaps
cards.trigger: DocumentBuilderFactory, SAXParserFactory, XMLInputFactory, SAXReader, TransformerFactory, XML upload/body, or setFeature misconfiguration.
cards.first_safe_check: Confirm XML parser reachability with a harmless malformed XML or local entity-disabled differential before external entity/file reads.
cards.oracle: XML parser-specific error, entity expansion behavior, safe local harness result, or SSRF/file-read side effect.
cards.stop_rule: Stop XXE after parser is not reachable or features clearly disable external entities under controlled test.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to SSRF/fetcher validation or file upload parser if XML is transformed but external entities are blocked.
cards.snippet: XXE requires parser reachability and feature-state proof, not just XML-looking input.
cards.falsify: XML is not parsed by target sink or entity features are disabled with no side channel.

cards.title: Java SSRF through URL/RestTemplate/WebClient fetcher
cards.trigger: RestTemplate, WebClient, HttpClient, URL, URI, openConnection, webhook/import/fetch/avatar/proxy endpoints, or URL validation clues.
cards.first_safe_check: Use one safe same-origin or controlled callback/loopback differential to prove fetcher reachability and validator behavior.
cards.oracle: Fetched content reflected, status/timing differential, callback, or fetcher exception class.
cards.stop_rule: Stop SSRF variants after two flat validator/fetcher probes without a new differential.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to URL parser bypass only if validation and fetcher disagree; otherwise inspect source/config for internal targets.
cards.snippet: Java SSRF depends on validator/fetcher disagreement and a useful internal/flag path.
cards.confirm: One controlled URL changes fetcher output/timing/callback as expected.
cards.falsify: No URL reaches fetcher or validator blocks before fetch with no bypass evidence.

cards.title: Java Struts2 OGNL gated branch
cards.trigger: Struts2 dependency, .action routes, struts.xml, devMode, OGNL errors, file upload interceptor, or content-type parser clues.
cards.first_safe_check: Confirm Struts2 route/interceptor/parser and run one harmless OGNL/content-type/error differential matched to the version/feature.
cards.oracle: Struts/OGNL-specific error, devMode info, action mapping behavior, or content-type parser differential.
cards.stop_rule: Stop historical S2 payloads without version/feature/interceptor/parser evidence.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to action parameter binding, file upload, or source-proven sinks when OGNL gates fail.
cards.snippet: Struts2 is actionable only through version/feature/parser gates, not .action alone.
cards.confirm: A safe feature-matched probe produces Struts/OGNL-specific differential.
cards.falsify: No affected route/interceptor/parser or flat responses to safe probes.

cards.title: Java archive extraction Zip Slip write primitive
cards.trigger: ZipInputStream, ZipEntry, JarEntry, TarArchiveEntry, archive upload/extract feature, or filename normalization logic.
cards.first_safe_check: Use a canary archive path that should remain inside extraction root and verify path normalization/readback before traversal writes.
cards.oracle: Canary extracted location, traversal rejection/acceptance, readback/static route, or filesystem evidence.
cards.stop_rule: Stop traversal write if canonical path checks prevent escape and no downstream consumer exists.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to upload filename/path traversal or file-read closure if extraction is safe but file handling remains controlled.
cards.snippet: Archive extraction needs canary normalization proof before destructive or webroot writes.
cards.confirm: Controlled archive entry affects extraction path beyond expected root or appears in canary readback.
cards.falsify: Canonical path checks enforce root and no consumer of stored content exists.

cards.title: Java file/resource path traversal to config or flag read
cards.trigger: FileInputStream, Files.read, Paths.get, ClassPathResource, ResourceUtils, ServletContext.getResource, download/view/template/static resource route.
cards.first_safe_check: Trace base directory and normalization then request a harmless known file within allowed area before one traversal differential.
cards.oracle: Known file readback, path error difference, canonicalization bypass, or local harness resolved path.
cards.stop_rule: Stop traversal variants if canonical root enforcement is proven and no alternate decoder/consumer exists.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to classpath resource/config read or upload/writeback if absolute filesystem traversal is blocked.
cards.snippet: Path traversal should close through flag/config/source reads, not endless encoding variants without path model.
cards.confirm: One controlled path changes read target or error as predicted by the path model.
cards.falsify: Path is fixed/whitelisted/canonicalized and no decoder mismatch exists.

cards.title: Java native serialization or XMLDecoder parser branch
cards.trigger: ObjectInputStream, readObject, readUnshared, XMLDecoder, rO0AB, aced0005, serialized cookie/body/file, or Java object parser error.
cards.first_safe_check: Confirm parser invocation with a benign malformed serialization/XMLDecoder object and observe parser-specific error before gadget depth.
cards.oracle: Object stream/XMLDecoder exception class, benign object accepted, timing/writeback, or local harness parser output.
cards.stop_rule: Stop gadgets if parser is unreachable, data shape is not controlled, or classpath/gadget support is absent.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to file upload/path/parser configuration if serialized bytes are only stored, not consumed.
cards.snippet: Parser invocation and data-shape proof come before any gadget chain.
cards.confirm: Benign malformed/valid object produces parser-specific behavior at the target sink.
cards.falsify: Serialized data never reaches parser or parser rejects before gadget-relevant processing.

cards.title: Java JSP/Servlet legacy route and EL surface mapping
cards.trigger: JSP files, WEB-INF/web.xml, @WebServlet, RequestDispatcher, JSTL/taglibs, EL expressions, Tomcat legacy app layout.
cards.first_safe_check: Map web.xml/JSP actions/forms/includes and identify request parameters reaching JSP EL, servlet dispatch, or file/resource sinks.
cards.oracle: Route map, reflected parameter, JSP error, include/forward behavior, or EL/render differential.
cards.stop_rule: Stop JSP EL assumptions when controlled data is HTML-escaped only and never evaluated/included.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to servlet file/path/auth logic or upload JSP write if JSP route mapping reveals a consumer.
cards.snippet: Legacy Java Web often solves through route/include/session logic rather than modern CVEs.
cards.confirm: Mapped servlet/JSP route consumes controlled request/session data with observable render/dispatch behavior.
cards.falsify: No controlled data reaches JSP/servlet consumers after route mapping.

cards.title: Java source/config leak to shortest closure chain
cards.trigger: Leaked source, backup archive, .git, Docker/config files, stack trace path, source map, or downloadable JAR/WAR.
cards.first_safe_check: Run safe archive/source triage, config map, Java map, dep risk, and flag/config grep before black-box payloads.
cards.oracle: Source route map, config key names, dependency versions, flag candidate, or direct credential/control-plane path.
cards.stop_rule: Stop broad black-box probing until the leaked source/config has been converted into top-3 route/sink hypotheses.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to artifact-first bytecode mapping if source is partial but JAR/WAR/classes are available.
cards.snippet: In Java Web CTFs, leaked source/config usually dominates black-box payload guessing.
cards.confirm: Leaked artifact yields route/config/dependency/sink evidence or a direct flag/config path.
cards.falsify: Artifact is unrelated/sample-only after runtime/path/version checks.
